max_events_per_user.name: Max events in context
max_events_per_user.hint: Maximum number of recent events injected into LLM context per user

max_profiles_per_user.name: Max profile entries
max_profiles_per_user.hint: Maximum number of profile key-value pairs allowed per user

max_event_keep.name: Max events stored
max_event_keep.hint: Maximum number of event logs kept in database per user (oldest auto-deleted)

skills_dir.name: Skills directory
skills_dir.hint: Path to skills directory (defaults to data/skills/). Each skill is a subfolder containing either SKILL.md (recommended, single-file YAML frontmatter) or the legacy manifest.json + instruction.md pair.

max_context_chars.name: Max context chars per user
max_context_chars.hint: Maximum characters of memory context injected per user (0 = unlimited). Truncates by category priority when exceeded

inject_categories.name: Auto-injected categories
inject_categories.hint: Profile categories pushed into the system prompt every turn. Other categories stay queryable via memory_query(category=...). Use ["*"] to inject every category (legacy behaviour). Default: ["basic"]

disabled_skills.name: Disabled skills
disabled_skills.hint: List of skill names to disable (won't be registered as LLM tools). Example: ["tarot_reading", "daily_fortune"]

enable_slash_commands.name: Enable slash commands
enable_slash_commands.hint: Allow users to trigger skills via /command (e.g. /tarot, /fortune). When disabled, skills can only be triggered by LLM.

webui_port.name: Memory WebUI port
webui_port.hint: Port for the memory management WebUI (0 = disabled, valid range: 1-65535). Example: 8765

webui_host.name: Memory WebUI host
webui_host.hint: Bind address for the memory WebUI. Use 127.0.0.1 for local-only access

webui_token.name: Memory WebUI token
webui_token.hint: Access token for the memory WebUI (leave empty for no authentication, only safe for local access)

memory_auditor_enabled.name: Memory auditor (active recall)
memory_auditor_enabled.hint: Run a separate fast-LLM call after each turn to extract any facts the main LLM missed. Costs one extra LLM call per user batch. Default off — enable if your main LLM is too conservative about calling memory_update. ⚠️ Privacy: enabling this sends the user's latest message AND up to 50 of their existing profile entries (nicknames, locations, relationships, etc.) to the auditor model — which may be a different vendor than your main LLM if you set memory_auditor_model_uuid. Make sure that vendor's data-handling policy is acceptable before turning this on.

memory_auditor_model_uuid.name: Memory auditor model
memory_auditor_model_uuid.hint: Leave empty (recommended) to use the configured default fast LLM. Pick a specific model only when you want to pin the auditor to it.

memory_auditor_skip_keywords.name: Memory auditor skip keywords
memory_auditor_skip_keywords.hint: If the user's latest message contains any of these, the auditor skips that turn entirely (privacy guard for messages that shouldn't be recorded).

memory_auditor_max_inflight.name: Memory auditor max concurrent calls
memory_auditor_max_inflight.hint: Maximum number of concurrent in-flight auditor LLM calls. When this many audits are already running, new turns are dropped (best-effort; auditor is an opt-in bonus layer). Lower = stricter, fewer rate-limit hits; higher = more thorough but more provider load.
